response: The internet originated in the 1960s with U.S. Defense Department-funded research, leading to ARPANET in 1969 and the adoption of TCP/IP protocols in 1983. Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web in 1989, and the 1993 Mosaic browser made it widely accessible, sparking the dot-com boom of the late 1990s—though many companies collapsed in 2001, leaving giants like Amazon and Google. The mid-2000s saw the rise of social media (Facebook, YouTube) and mobile internet (iPhone, 2007), while cloud computing (AWS, 2006) revolutionized data storage. Today, the internet connects over **5 billion people**, shaping global commerce, communication, and daily life.
